## [00:00 - 03:05]

Hi, this is Don Crowler with a video about your dress rehearsal. So we've talked about rehearsing your webinar, but then there's one more rehearsal that is absolutely essential, and that is your dress rehearsal, which needs to happen at the latest the day before your webinar. Why is that? Because if there are things that you've got to fix, you may need more time. So the dress rehearsal is a live webinar on the platform you'll be performing on. So this is the equivalent of go to the stage you're going to be speaking on and give it there. It needs to be attended by you, your guests, if you're going to have any guests on this, any support people that you're going to have, and someone with the other kind of cell phone plan than you have. Now let me explain what I mean by that last fact. One of the things we're going to be doing is we're going to be testing your any URLs you're going to give to this, including your sales page URL. You let's say you're an iPhone person, great. So you go through it, you test that you show the button, you click on the button, everything works fine on your iPhone. Well, guess what? What about Android? You need to have somebody on this call who has an Android phone that you can push the buy button to if your webinar program supports that, or who can key in the URL for it, and just make sure that everything works on the Android device too. In fact, I actually recommend you have somebody with an iPhone, an iPad, an Android phone, an Android pad. Hey, while we're at it, a desktop PC and a desktop Mac. So you've got basically the majority of everyone who's going to be watching you, you've got a way of testing it live in that environment to ensure that everything actually works. You can't assume that things are going to work. That's why we do dress rehearsals. So during this dress rehearsal, you're going to test your microphones. You're going to test screen share. So what you're seeing on the screen right here, you're going to test practice making that happen, and make sure that everything works. You're going to, and part of that screen share, you need to click through every single slide to make sure all your animations are right. You're going to test any live videos. So if you are going to, let's say show a video of someone giving a testimonial, you need to test that live to make sure they can hear the sound, you've got it right, and that they can see movement of the person.

## [03:05 - 06:07]

Every single little piece needs to be tested. You're going to test your chat functionality. All right, how is chat working, the ability for people to place questions to do chat, the ability to take questions via chat, the ability to take questions via voice. Now you may say, Oh, I'm not going to allow anybody to come on and ask me a question live. Well, I always want to test it, because I may decide to do that at some point, including a person who's asking a question. But I also want to may bring my support person on there for a moment. If they've got something. So even if I don't intend on using it, I'm going to test it so I know how it works. So I know how to do it. And so I know that it's just there in case I get this bright idea and decide to do it, you're going to test your recording. You're going to test that your recording actually works afterwards by testing the playback on it. You're going to test any downloads to make sure that they work. You're going to obviously test your sales page. And every page in your sales page process, the full funnel to make sure every single element of that works, you're going to test your countdown timers to make sure that those work any links either clickable or otherwise, as well as your process of putting those links up on the screen. In other words, I've given you a pre exhaustive list here, but you're probably going to come up with a couple of things that aren't on this list. You need to test everything that's going to happen on the webinar and make sure that it works. Now, everything I just said is the test that you do if you're comfortable and have used your webinars technology a bunch of times. If this is your first webinar or your first webinar with new technology, you may need another dress rehearsal before this one, just to make sure that you everything works and you know how it works. And you've got time to talk to their support people if you need to your goal in this entire process is literally to achieve perfection. You want your webinar to go off technically pure with not a single bobble, not a single mistake, so that the only thing that it resides on the only thing you're leaving to chance is the words and the tone of voice that comes out of your mouth. Let's take every piece off the table we can, every opportunity for mistakes, eliminate those in the dress rehearsal, so that you've got something that you know is just going to work. Now, while you're doing that, I also recommend that you rehearse your offer section of the webinar. So start at the pivot and go word for word

## [06:08 - 07:14]

all the way to the end. Don't skip any parts. So yes, this dress rehearsal, you've got 45 minutes of going through the offer portion of your webinar that you're going to give word for word, and you're going to have the people who are on that call paying attention, taking notes, and giving you feedback on how and what you said everything throughout that entire process. So from the pivot to the webinar close, I guarantee you do this, you're going to present better as a result. And I strongly suggest you record this process and watch it. And you may need to do that several times. So you make sure you get every word of your sales offer exactly the way you want it to be. Now I can make a point here. Yes, this requires real discipline, a discipline that, hey, our industry is not necessarily well known for having. But I strongly suggest you do it, because it'll pay off.
